DISCUSSION AT TIPPERARY CO.

At_a meeting of Tipperary Co. Board
the Secretary ‘read a’ letter from the

the matter had been referred to the
County Board, the local clergy to be
aoked to-attend. The Secretary said he
thought it better not to invite the clergy
until instructed by the County Board.

Mr. Mason read a report of the pro-
ceedings at the joint meeting held in
Thurles at Mr, Walsh's a couple of
months ago. He said he was one of
three delegates of the Couny Board
appointed to attend at the joint meeting
above ae i, & Cr calogaiee

R. O'Leary, Cashel. When he entered

see himself the only delegate present.
The Rev. M. K. Ryan presided, and a
discussion began as to the value of the
old Hall in Thurles, which was in ruins,
but not a word was said about the site
for the memorial. He (Mr. Mason) said.
the meeting was to decide as to the
erection of a memorial in ‘Thurles, but
Father Ryan said the Hall was in Thur-
les, and that the memorial could take
any shape. Mr. Mason said he didn't
understand about the Hall, but that his
instructions were that a statue to Dr.
Croke was to be erected in The Square
in Thurles. A discussion then arose as
tothe boundaries or limits of The
Square, and various suggestions were
made as to the position of the memorial,
some delegates referring to, the College
grounds, others favouring a Hall fronting
the Main Street, but he (Mr. Mason)
contended all the time that he had no
‘authority from his Board to agree to any-
